skill.name: backend-hang-debug
skill.description: Diagnose FastAPI hangs caused by blocking ThreadPoolExecutor shutdown in SSE stream routes, using local triage and safer executor cleanup patterns.
skill.summary: Debug FastAPI stream hangs and apply non-blocking executor shutdown patterns.
skill.author: BenderFendor

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, and filesystem patterns, but they are Markdown triage instructions rather than executable skill code. The sudo py-spy and /proc process-inspection steps are legitimate for backend debugging but create elevated operational risk and should be reviewed before use. No prompt injection attempt, credential exfiltration, or malicious payload was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: Privileged Process Stack Inspection
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to install py-spy and run it with sudo against uvicorn worker processes. This is a legitimate hang-debugging technique, but it requires elevated privileges and can expose process memory or stack details from the local service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The sudo command and py-spy dump target are explicit on the cited line. The surrounding context confirms debugging intent, so this is operationally risky but not clearly malicious.

medium_findings.title: Local Process and Filesystem Reconnaissance
medium_findings.description: The triage flow asks users to enumerate listeners and inspect /proc file descriptors for a uvicorn process. This can reveal local process state and file descriptor information, but it is scoped to diagnosing a backend hang.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The /proc and listener inspection commands are directly present. They are common diagnostics, but they still collect local process metadata.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Localhost Network Checks
low_findings.description: The skill includes curl checks against localhost health and root endpoints. These are local smoke tests, not external network exfiltration, so the static network findings are low risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are localhost-only and appear in verification steps. No evidence found of external transmission or credential handling.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detection
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at the skill description, but the cited text describes FastAPI hangs and ThreadPoolExecutor shutdown. No cryptographic operation is present there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains descriptive prose only. No evidence found of hashing, encryption, or crypto API usage.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Detected as Execution
low_findings.description: Most external command findings are inline Markdown examples for curl, py-spy, executor cleanup text, and verification steps. They are instructions for a human or agent, not code that executes automatically when the skill loads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown with diagnostic instructions only. No script file, hook, or automatic execution path was found in the scanned file.

dangerous_patterns.title: sudo Diagnostic Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill recommends running py-spy with sudo against a process selected by pgrep. This should only be used in trusted local development or controlled production-debug sessions.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The elevated command is explicit and includes command substitution for process selection. Context shows a debugging purpose rather than persistence or exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Substitution in Process Targeting
dangerous_patterns.description: The py-spy and /proc examples use command substitution with pgrep to select uvicorn processes. This is useful for diagnostics but can target the wrong process if the pattern matches unexpectedly.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command substitution syntax is directly present. The practical risk depends on the local process list and user permissions.

content.value_statement: FastAPI stream routes can freeze when executor shutdown blocks the event loop. This skill guides triage, stack capture, and safer cleanup patterns.
actual_capabilities: Guides reproduction of FastAPI root and health endpoint hangs with timed localhost checks.
Shows how to inspect uvicorn listener state and file descriptor counts during a hang.
Uses py-spy stack dumps to identify ThreadPoolExecutor shutdown blocking the event loop.
Recommends replacing context-managed executors with explicit non-blocking shutdown.
Provides verification checks for stream aborts, health responses, and responsive frontend behavior.
limitations: Focused on FastAPI SSE stream hangs caused by executor shutdown behavior.
Requires local shell access to the backend runtime and process list.
Some diagnostics may require elevated privileges for stack inspection.
Does not replace broader profiling, tracing, or incident response tooling.

use_cases.title: Investigate Frozen API Health Checks
use_cases.description: Reproduce local health endpoint timeouts and identify whether the event loop is blocked.

use_cases.title: Capture Evidence During Production Debugging
use_cases.description: Use controlled stack capture to confirm whether uvicorn workers are waiting in executor shutdown.

use_cases.title: Review Stream Cleanup Changes
use_cases.description: Validate that SSE disconnect handling cancels pending futures and keeps the service responsive.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Suspected Hang
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner triage for a local backend that stops responding.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the backend-hang-debug skill to help me check why my FastAPI service stops responding on root and health endpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Safe Stack Capture
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare diagnostics before running privileged tooling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to plan a safe py-spy stack capture for a uvicorn worker. Include permission risks and what evidence to collect.

prompt_templates.title: Fix Executor Shutdown
prompt_templates.scenario: Apply the non-blocking executor cleanup pattern.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill to update a FastAPI SSE route that uses ThreadPoolExecutor. Keep shutdown non-blocking and handle client disconnects.

prompt_templates.title: Audit the Full Debugging Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of diagnosis, fix, and verification.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my hang investigation, stack evidence, executor changes, and verification checklist for gaps.

output_examples.input: My FastAPI health endpoint times out after a stream client disconnects.
output: A short triage sequence that confirms the listener is active and health checks are timing out.
A stack-capture plan focused on uvicorn worker processes and event-loop blocking frames.
A likely root cause section naming blocking executor shutdown as the first hypothesis.

output_examples.input: Review my proposed stream route cleanup change.
output: A checklist for pending future cancellation, non-blocking shutdown, and disconnect handling.
A note explaining why waiting for worker threads can freeze the event loop.
A retest plan covering health responses, aborted streams, and stack dump confirmation.

output_examples.input: Prepare an incident note for a backend hang investigation.
output: A concise timeline of symptoms, diagnostics, suspected cause, and mitigation.
A risk note for privileged stack inspection and local process metadata exposure.
Follow-up hardening items for request timeouts and RSS source retry behavior.

best_practices: Run privileged stack inspection only in trusted environments with approval.
Capture evidence before changing executor behavior so the fix targets the confirmed blocker.
Verify both stream abort behavior and unrelated health endpoints after the change.
anti_patterns: Do not run sudo diagnostics on shared systems without reviewing the target process first.
Do not assume every FastAPI hang is caused by ThreadPoolExecutor shutdown.
Do not remove timeouts or cancellation handling while changing stream cleanup logic.

faq.question: What problem does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on FastAPI hangs caused by blocking ThreadPoolExecutor shutdown inside SSE stream handling.

faq.question: Does the skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. The scanned file contains Markdown instructions and examples, not executable scripts or hooks.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill recommends sudo stack inspection and process metadata checks. These are useful but sensitive diagnostics.

faq.question: Can I use it without py-spy?
faq.answer: You can still review executor shutdown logic, but stack evidence is weaker without a live stack dump.

faq.question: Is it only for local development?
faq.answer: It is safest in local or controlled environments. Production use needs approval and careful process targeting.

faq.question: What should I verify after applying the fix?
faq.answer: Verify health endpoint responses, aborted stream behavior, and absence of blocking shutdown frames in the main thread.
